title: Enterprise PM — Customer Journey: ProductOS Adoption

journey_stages.description: PM does not realize they have a research compilation problem. They think spending 15hrs/week on manual competitive research, status updates, and strategy doc compilation is just part of the job.
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: This is just how PM work is done. Every PM I know spends hours on manual research.
pain_points: No awareness that AI tooling can reduce 15hrs/week of manual work
Assumes all PMs operate this way — no urgency to change

journey_stages.description: PM sees a peer using AI PM tooling and realizes they are spending 15hrs/week on tasks that could be automated. Initial curiosity triggered.
persona_actions: Searches for AI PM tools on ProductHunt
Joins PM AI tooling Slack community
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: Wait, Sarah from the PM community cut her discovery time by 70%? What am I doing wrong?
pain_points: Hard to distinguish real capability from hype — every tool claims to be AI-powered
No trusted source for AI PM tooling comparison

journey_stages.description: PM actively evaluates 3-4 AI PM tooling options. Reads G2 and Capterra reviews, watches demo videos, asks peer PMs in Slack communities for honest feedback.
persona_actions: Posts in PM Slack asking for recommendations
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: I need something that actually saves time — not another tool that adds overhead. The real test is output quality compared to what I produce manually.
pain_points: Inconsistent review quality — hard to compare depth across tools
Demo videos show happy path only — do not reveal edge cases or failure modes

journey_stages.description: PM narrows to 2 options. Signs up for trials. Compares output quality against their own manual work. The first generated competitive analysis and PRD draft are the critical evaluation points.
persona_actions: Compares AI output side-by-side with manual output
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: This is the real test. If the AI output is 80% as good as my manual work, I will recommend this to my VP. If it is below 60%, I will abandon.
pain_points: 14-day trial is too short to test quarterly workflow
Output quality comparison is manual and subjective — no built-in benchmark

journey_stages.description: PM selects ProductOS based on Jira integration depth and evidence traceability. Prepares business case with ROI quantification for VP approval.
persona_actions: Quantifies ROI: hours saved vs cost
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: I am confident this will save 10hrs/week. But I need to prove it with numbers the VP will believe — not just my enthusiasm.
pain_points: Need to quantify ROI for VP approval — no built-in ROI calculator in the tool
Stakeholder-specific messaging requires separate documents

journey_stages.description: VP approves purchase. Procurement process begins: legal review, security questionnaire (45 questions), SOC2 compliance verification. Three separate processes, each asking for different documentation.
persona_actions: Follows up on stuck procurement steps
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: Why is this taking so long? The security questionnaire is 45 questions and the vendor docs do not have pre-filled answers. This should not take 4 weeks.
pain_points: Security questionnaire is 45 questions — PM has to pull answers from vendor docs manually
Legal review finds gaps in compliance documentation — delays 2 weeks

journey_stages.description: PM sets up workspace. Ingests first batch of research documents. First generated output is promising but needs refinement — the PRD is too shallow and persona descriptions feel generic.
persona_actions: Uploads first batch of research files
Reviews and edits first AI-generated outputs
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: The first output is promising but not production-ready. I still need to rewrite about 30%. But the structure is there — I am not starting from blank anymore.
pain_points: Learning curve steeper than expected — need to understand artifact dependency graph
First PRD is too shallow — need to configure maturity band and depth settings

journey_stages.description: PM integrates ProductOS into weekly workflow. Auto-generated status mail saves 3hrs/week. Strategy deck first draft saves 8hrs/quarter. PM becomes dependent on the tool for routine PM tasks.
persona_actions: Uses ProductOS for strategy deck first drafts
Configures competitive radar for automated monitoring
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: I just saved 3 hours this week on status updates alone. The strategy deck first draft was 70% there — I spent 2 hours refining instead of 10 hours building from scratch.
pain_points: Occasional output that feels AI-generated — generic persona descriptions that need rewriting
Still some manual cleanup needed for stakeholder-facing outputs

journey_stages.description: PM brings their 2 APMs onto ProductOS. Creates shared workspace. Team velocity improves — stories now have auto-generated acceptance criteria. PM becomes internal champion.
persona_actions: Integrates ProductOS into team sprint planning
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: My APMs are getting up to speed faster. Stories are less ambiguous. Sprint carry-over is dropping. This is actually working at team scale.
pain_points: Permission model needs work — APMs can accidentally modify canonical artifacts
Would like approval workflow for team contributions before they reach canonical state

journey_stages.description: PM recommends ProductOS in PM Slack community. Writes G2 and Capterra reviews. Presents at internal PM all-hands about cutting discovery time by 70%. Becomes reference customer.
persona_actions: Recommends ProductOS in PM Slack communities
Presents case study at internal all-hands
Agrees to be a reference customer
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: I genuinely believe this makes PMs better. I want other PMs to experience the same leverage I am getting. Plus, being the PM who brought this tool to the team is good for my career.
pain_points: No referral program or incentive for advocacy
No built-in case study template — advocacy content written from scratch

journey_stages.description: Annual renewal approaches. PM evaluates if value delivered matches price. Reviews output quality trend over past year. Assesses if competitors have caught up. Renewal decision depends on: output quality improvement trajectory, feature roadmap delivery (V10?), and integration stability.
persona_actions: Compares current output quality to manual baseline
Makes renewal recommendation to VP Product
journey_stages.persona_thoughts: ProductOS saved me about 400 hours this year. But competitor X just launched a similar tool at half the price. If V10 ships with the prototype generation and dashboard they promised, I will renew. If not, I will evaluate alternatives.
pain_points: ROI quantification is manual — would like auto-generated value delivered report
Feature roadmap delivery is key renewal driver but roadmap visibility is limited

overall_emotion_curve.curve_description: U-shaped journey: high satisfaction during evaluation and adoption, deep valley during procurement/security review, recovery during onboarding and adoption

moments_of_truth.description: First generated PRD output — PM compares quality against their manual work. If output is >80% parity, they proceed. If <60%, they abandon.
Security review questionnaire — if documentation is incomplete or compliance gaps exist, deal stalls for 2-4 weeks. Enterprise buyers may walk if gaps are material.
First 7 days of usage — PM needs to reach 'time-to-first-value' within 1 week or adoption stalls. Key value moment: first auto-generated competitive analysis.

gap_analysis.current_vs_ideal_summary: Current journey: strong evaluation and long-term adoption, but procurement/security review creates a 4-week valley that loses 15% of qualified deals. Ideal journey: automated security documentation + pre-built compliance packs reduce purchase stage to 1 week.

critical_gaps.description: Security review documentation insufficient — enterprise buyers blocked on incomplete compliance information
critical_gaps.impact_on_customer: 4-week delay in deployment. 15% drop-off during security review stage.

unmet_expectations: PM expected 'one-click deployment' after procurement — actual setup requires workspace configuration and artifact dependency understanding

opportunities.title: Auto-Generated Security Documentation
opportunities.description: Generate SOC2 compliance pack, security questionnaire answers, and architecture diagram automatically from product artifacts

opportunities.title: 7-Day Onboarding Accelerator
opportunities.description: Pre-configured workspace template with 7-day guided workflow that produces first usable competitive analysis by day 3

opportunities.title: ROI Value Report
opportunities.description: Auto-generated quarterly value report: hours saved, artifacts generated, PM leverage increase since adoption
